name: Vutrisiran reduced ATTR-CM death and cardiovascular events
summary: HELIOS-B strengthens disease-outcome evidence for TTR-lowering in ATTR-CM, including all-cause death and recurrent cardiovascular events.
statement: In ATTR cardiomyopathy, vutrisiran lowered the risk of death from any cause and recurrent cardiovascular events and preserved functional capacity and quality of life.
population_or_model: Patients with ATTR cardiomyopathy, with and without baseline tafamidis
time_horizon: Up to 36 months for the primary endpoint and 42 months for all-cause death analysis
quantitative_note: The primary composite hazard ratio was 0.72 in the overall population (95% CI 0.56 to 0.93; P=0.01), and all-cause death through 42 months had HR 0.65 (95% CI 0.46 to 0.90; P=0.01); 6-minute-walk difference was 26.5 m and KCCQ-OS difference was 5.8 points.
caveats: Vutrisiran suppresses hepatic TTR production rather than directly removing deposited amyloid.
The result is highly relevant to age-associated amyloid cardiomyopathy but not proof of broad aggregate clearance across aging tissues.
The trial allowed background tafamidis, requiring careful interpretation of monotherapy versus add-on contexts.
